title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruption, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and multi-factor authentication (MFA) for CI/CD pipelines and cloud accounts.
Conduct regular security audits of CI/CD pipelines to identify and patch vulnerabilities.
Deploy robust backup and disaster recovery solutions to ensure data can be restored in the event of an attack.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal risk, but widespread adoption without proper vetting could lead to false positives, reliance on unverified patches, or exploitation by malicious actors if the tool is compromised.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and credibility before adoption.
Use the scanner as a supplementary tool alongside established vulnerability management processes.
Monitor the tool's updates and community feedback for potential security concerns.

title: Improper handling of insufficient privileges in the AMD Secure Processor (ASP) could allow an...

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2025-54511.

title: Insufficient parameter sanitization in TEE SOC Driver could allow an attacker to issue a...
description: Insufficient parameter sanitization in TEE SOC Driver could allow an attacker to issue a malformed DRV_SOC_CMD_ID_SRIOV_CHECK_TA_COMPAT to cause incorrect shared memory mapping, potentially resulting in unexpected behavior.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the TEE SOC Driver could allow attackers to exploit insufficient parameter sanitization, leading to incorrect shared memory mapping. This may result in unexpected behavior, such as system instability or potential data corruption, but the severity is considered low due to limited exploitability.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches provided by the vendor to address CVE-2025-66660.
Conduct a thorough code review to ensure proper parameter sanitization in SOC drivers.
Implement runtime memory mapping validation to detect and prevent incorrect configurations.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2025-48513.

title: Rapid7 Metasploit Pro is vulnerable to a local privilege escalation attack that allows users to...
description: Rapid7 Metasploit Pro is vulnerable to a local privilege escalation attack that allows users to gain SYSTEM level control of a Windows host. Upon startup the metasploitPostgreSQL service the subsequent  postgres.exe service attempts to load an OpenSSL configuration file from a non-existent directory that is writable by standard users. By planting a crafted openssl.cnf file an attacker can trick the high-privilege service into executing arbitrary commands. This effectively permits an unprivileged

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7373.

title: Improper access control between the Joint Test Action Group (JTAG) and Advanced Extensible...
description: Improper access control between the Joint Test Action Group (JTAG) and Advanced Extensible Interface (AXI) could allow an attacker with physical access to read or overwrite the contents of cross-chip debug (XCD) registers potentially resulting in loss of data integrity or confidentiality.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2025-0040.

title: Insufficient parameter sanitization in AMD Secure Processor (ASP) TEE SOC Driver could allow an...
description: Insufficient parameter sanitization in AMD Secure Processor (ASP) TEE SOC Driver could allow an attacker to issue a malformed DRV_SOC_CMD_ID_LOAD_GFX_IP_FW SR-IOV command to cause out-of-bounds read, potentially resulting in SOC Driver memory contents exposure or an exception

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the AMD Secure Processor TEE SOC Driver could allow attackers to exploit insufficient parameter sanitization, leading to out-of-bounds reads. This may expose sensitive SOC Driver memory contents or cause exceptions, potentially compromising system integrity and data confidentiality.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches or firmware updates provided by AMD to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization in applications interacting with the SOC Driver to prevent malformed commands.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection mechanisms to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: An out of bounds read within the AMD Platform Management Framework (PMF) could allow an attacker...
description: An out of bounds read within the AMD Platform Management Framework (PMF) could allow an attacker to trigger a read of an arbitrary memory location potentially resulting in loss of availability or confidentiality.

Conduct a thorough review of systems utilizing AMD Platform Management Framework to identify potential exposure.
Implement monitoring tools to detect and respond to unusual memory access patterns.

title: An out-of-bounds read in power management firmware by a malicious local attacker with low...
description: An out-of-bounds read in power management firmware by a malicious local attacker with low privileges could potentially lead to a partial loss of confidentiality and availability.

This could expose sensitive data or disrupt system operations.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest firmware updates or patches provided by the vendor to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict local access to trusted personnel and enforce strict access control policies.
Monitor system logs for unusual activity that may indicate exploitation attempts.

title: A vulnerability in mlflow/mlflow versions 3.9.0 and earlier allows unauthenticated access to...
description: A vulnerability in mlflow/mlflow versions 3.9.0 and earlier allows unauthenticated access to certain FastAPI routes when the server is started with authentication enabled (`--app-name basic-auth`) and served via uvicorn (ASGI). The FastAPI permission middleware only enforces authentication on `/gateway/` routes, leaving other routes such as the Job API (`/ajax-api/3.0/jobs/*`) and the OpenTelemetry trace ingestion API (`/v1/traces`) unprotected. This allows unauthenticated remote attackers to su

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote attackers to access sensitive APIs, potentially leading to unauthorized data access, job manipulation, or telemetry data exfiltration. This could compromise system integrity and expose sensitive information.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to a patched version of mlflow/mlflow that addresses CVE-2026-2652.
Implement additional network-level access controls (e.g., IP whitelisting or VPN) to restrict access to the affected APIs.
Conduct a thorough review of server logs to identify any unauthorized access attempts or suspicious activity.

description: An unchecked return value within the AMD Platform Management Framework (PMF)  could allow an attacker to read or modify an arbitrary address potentially resulting in loss of confidentiality, integrity, or availability.

This could result in unauthorized data access, system compromise, or denial of service.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches provided by AMD as soon as they become available.
Implement strict access controls and monitoring to detect and prevent unauthorized access to affected systems.
Conduct a thorough review of systems using AMD hardware to identify and mitigate potential exploitation vectors.

description: Improper cleanup of shared register resources in GPU firmware could allow an admin-privileged attacker from a Guest Virtual machine (VM) to access these shared resources from another Guest VM, potentially resulting in the loss of confidentiality, integrity, or availability.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-0427.

title: WWW::Mechanize::Cached versions before 2.00 for Perl deserialize cached HTTP responses from a...
description:
WWW::Mechanize::Cached versions before 2.00 for Perl deserialize cached HTTP responses from a world-writable on-disk cache, enabling local response forgery and code execution.

With no explicit cache backend, WWW::Mechanize::Cached constructs a default Cache::FileCache under /tmp/FileCache without overriding the backend's documented directory_umask of 000, so the cache root and its subdirectories are created mode 0777 with no sticky bit. Cache entries are named by sha1_hex of the request and rea

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8612.

title: Improper isolation of GPU HW register space could allow a privileged attacker in malicious Guest...
description: Improper isolation of GPU HW register space could allow a privileged attacker in malicious Guest Virtual Machine (VM) to perform unauthorized access to specific victim range of GPU MMIO register space, potentially causing the host OS to reboot and creating a Denial of Service (DOS) condition.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows a privileged attacker in a malicious Guest VM to access GPU MMIO register space improperly, potentially causing the host OS to reboot and resulting in a Denial of Service (DOS) condition. This could disrupt critical operations and affect system availability.
recommended_actions: Apply patches or updates from the vendor addressing CVE-2024-36332 as soon as they are available.
Restrict access to virtual machines and ensure proper privilege management to minimize the risk of malicious Guest VMs.
Monitor GPU-related activity and logs for unusual behavior that could indicate exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2026-41498 - Kimai is an open-source time tracking application. Prior to version 2.54.0, the Team API endpoints u
description: Kimai is an open-source time tracking application. Prior to version 2.54.0, the Team API endpoints use #[IsGranted('edit_team')] instead of #[IsGranted('edit', 'team')], causing Symfony TeamVoter to abstain from voting. This removes entity-level ownership checks on team operations, allowing any user with the edit_team permission to modify any team, not just teams they are authorized to manage. This issue has been patched in version 2.54.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows users with the 'edit_team' permission to modify any team, bypassing entity-level ownership checks. This could lead to unauthorized changes to team configurations, potentially causing data integrity issues or unauthorized access to sensitive team-related information.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Kimai to version 2.54.0 or later to apply the patch addressing this vulnerability.
Review and audit user permissions to ensure that only trusted users have the 'edit_team' permission.
Monitor logs for any unauthorized or unusual team modification activities to detect potential exploitation.

title: CVE-2026-8128 - A vulnerability was found in SourceCodester SUP Online Shopping 1.0. The affected element is an unkn
description: A vulnerability was found in SourceCodester SUP Online Shopping 1.0. The affected element is an unknown function of the file /admin/viewmsg.php. Performing a manipulation of the argument msgid results in sql injection. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8128.

title: CVE-2026-8127 - A vulnerability has been found in eladmin up to 2.7. Impacted is the function checkLevel of the file
description: A vulnerability has been found in eladmin up to 2.7. Impacted is the function checkLevel of the file /rest/UserController.java of the component Users API Endpoint. Such manipulation leads to improper access controls. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8127.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8126.

title: CVE-2026-6737 - An Exposed IOCTL with Insufficient Access Control vulnerability in AsusPTPFilter allows a local user
description:
An Exposed IOCTL with Insufficient Access Control vulnerability in AsusPTPFilter allows a local user to bypass driver security mechanisms and obtain restricted touchpad information or render the touchpad unusable via crafted IOCTL requests.Refer to the '
Security Update for ASUS Precision Touchpad ' section on the ASUS Security Advisory for more information.

title: CVE-2026-3508 - An Out-of-bounds Read vulnerability in the IOCTL handler in ASUS System Control Interface allows a l
description:
An Out-of-bounds Read vulnerability in the IOCTL handler in ASUS System Control Interface allows a local user to cause system crash (BSOD) via a read size that exceeds the buffer size.Refer to the '
Security Update for MyASUS ' section on the ASUS Security Advisory for more information.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3508.

description: A vulnerability was detected in code-projects Simple Chat System 1.0. This vulnerability affects unknown code of the file sendMessage.php. title: CVE-2026-8124 - A security vulnerability has been detected in GPAC up to 26.02.0. This affects the function sidx_box
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in GPAC up to 26.02.0. This affects the function sidx_box_read of the file src/isomedia/box_code_base.c. The manipulation leads to allocation of resources. The attack must be carried out locally. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The identifier of the patch is 442e2299530138d8f874fd885c565ba98a6318ba. It is suggested to install a patch to address this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8124.

description: A vulnerability was determined in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. This impacts the function ogs_sbi_discovery_option_add_snssais in the library /lib/sbi/message.c of the component NSSF.
